reasoning: The September 2025 FOMC meeting resulted in a 25 basis point rate cut, fulfilling the first condition. CME FedWatch data from late October (just prior to the October 26 forecast date) indicates near-certainty (~98-100% probability) for another rate cut at the October 28-29 FOMC meeting, which would satisfy the second condition. However, following recent cooling inflation data in October, market expectations for the December meeting have shifted overwhelmingly towards a third consecutive cut. As of October 25, the CME FedWatch tool placed a 98.5% probability on a December rate cut, leaving essentially a ~1.5% implied chance of a pause. While economic surprises (like hotter-than-expected inflation or blowout jobs data in November) could still force a pause, the overwhelming market consensus points to a Cut-Cut-Cut sequence. I am predicting 3% to account for the very slim chance of a December pause.
